seo.title: Building AI Chatbot with LlamaIndex + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai chatbot with LlamaIndex + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Chatbot with LlamaIndex enables powerful retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) by connecting LLMs to external data sources through indexing and retrieval. However, conversational agents face risks like irrelevant retrievals, hallucinations, and coherence issues across multi-turn interactions. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify response accuracy, safety, and fidelity to source documents, ensuring reliable and production-ready chatbot performance.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Chatbot Architecture with LlamaIndex
architectureOverview.description:
The typical LlamaIndex AI Chatbot architecture starts with data ingestion, where documents are chunked, embedded, and stored in a vector index. User queries trigger intent classification, followed by retrieval of relevant context from the index using the query engine. The retrieved context is fed into a synthesizer or chat engine with conversation memory for multi-turn coherence, generating responses via an LLM like GPT-4.

Key components include the VectorStoreIndex for semantic search, a RouterQueryEngine for intent routing, and ChatMemoryBuffer for maintaining conversation history. Safety layers filter PII and harmful content before final output. This RAG pattern enhances factual accuracy but introduces failure modes like chunk boundary splits and index staleness.

Multi-turn memory management uses buffers to limit context window overflow, while retrievers dynamically fetch fresh context per query.

risks.name: Retrieved context irrelevant to query
risks.description: Vector search returns semantically similar but factually unrelated chunks, leading to hallucinated or off-topic responses.
risks.mitigation: Use Vex to score response relevance against retrieved nodes and reroute low-confidence queries.

risks.name: Synthesizer hallucinating beyond source documents
risks.description: LLM generates plausible but unsupported claims by extrapolating beyond provided context.
risks.mitigation: Vex citation verification ensures every claim traces back to source documents with exact matches.

risks.name: Index staleness causing outdated responses
risks.description: Documents change but index remains static, serving expired information in conversations.
risks.mitigation: Implement Vex freshness checks and automated reindexing triggers based on document metadata.

risks.name: Chunk boundary errors losing critical context
risks.description: Document splitting creates chunks that cut mid-sentence, breaking factual continuity.
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic continuity scoring flags responses derived from incomplete context chunks.

implementation.title: Basic LlamaIndex Query Engine Setup
implementation.description: Initialize VectorStoreIndex and create a query engine for RAG retrieval.
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader, Settings
from llama_index.llms.openai import OpenAI

# Configure LLM and embeddings
Settings.llm = OpenAI(model="gpt-4")
Settings.embed_model = "text-embedding-3-small"

documents = SimpleDirectoryReader("data").load_data()
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(documents)
query_engine = index.as_query_engine(similarity_top_k=3)

response = query_engine.query("What is the latest product pricing?")
print(response)

implementation.title: Chat Engine with Memory for Multi-Turn
implementation.description: Create a chat engine with conversation memory buffer for coherent multi-turn interactions.
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core.memory import ChatMemoryBuffer
from llama_index.core.chat_engine import CondenseQuestionChatEngine

memory = ChatMemoryBuffer.from_defaults(token_limit=1500)
chat_engine = CondenseQuestionChatEngine.from_defaults(
    query_engine, memory=memory
)

response = chat_engine.chat("Hi, tell me about pricing")
print(response)
response = chat_engine.chat("What changed since last quarter?")
print(response)

implementation.title: TypeScript LlamaIndex Node Query Setup
implementation.description: Initialize LlamaIndex in Node.js with OpenAI embeddings for server-side chatbot.

implementation.title: Router Query Engine for Intent Routing
implementation.description: Route queries to specialized query engines based on intent classification.
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core.query_engine import RouterQueryEngine
from llama_index.core.selectors import LLMSingleSelector
from llama_index.core.tools import QueryEngineTool

pricing_engine = pricing_index.as_query_engine()
support_engine = support_index.as_query_engine()

query_engine_tools = [
    QueryEngineTool.from_defaults(pricing_engine, name="pricing", description="Pricing questions"),
    QueryEngineTool.from_defaults(support_engine, name="support", description="Technical support")
]

router_engine = RouterQueryEngine(
    selector=LLMSingleSelector.from_defaults(),
    query_engine_tools=query_engine_tools
)

response = router_engine.query("How much does enterprise cost?")
print(response)
